species: The paralogues RrpA and RrpB, which are members of the MarR family of DNA binding proteins, are important for the survival of the global bacterial foodborne pathogen Campylobacter jejuni under redox stress.

strain: Disruption of rrpA, rrpB, and rrpAB shows altered transcription profiles in C. jejuni 11168H.
